deck: 31 cards — Neutralize+, Survivor, 4x Strike, 5x Defend (1 upgraded), After Image+, Blade Dance, Quick Slash, Flying Knee, Infinite Blades, Flash of Steel, Finisher, Well-Laid Plans, Tools of the Trade, Accuracy, Sneaky Strike, Corpse Explosion, Footwork, Noxious Fumes, Blur, Adrenaline, Reflex, Backflip+, Deflect+, Catalyst+, Parasite (curse)
relics: Ring of the Snake, Bird-Faced Urn, Wing Boots, Regal Pillow, Fusion Hammer, Enchiridion, Letter Opener, Sozu, War Paint
cause_of_death: Awakened One Phase 2, Turn 14. 6 HP vs 16x3 = 48 multi-attack with 28 block. AO at 188/300 HP with Regenerate 10.
key_moments: First Silent run reached Act 3 boss. Poison + shiv archetype.

Played 6 Power cards into Awakened One (After Image+, Footwork, Infinite Blades, Noxious Fumes, Accuracy, Tools of Trade), giving AO +6 Str minimum across phases. Player declared powers "essential" and ignored Curiosity mechanic.
The +6 Str turned a survivable 10x3 = 30 multi-attack into lethal 16x3 = 48. With 28 block and 6 HP, the extra Str from Powers was the exact margin of death.
Catalyst+ was exhausted (poison tripling is one-shot), AO down to 188/300 but Regenerate 10 healing each turn.
lessons: Awakened One Curiosity mechanic is the CENTRAL consideration of the fight, not a footnote. Playing Powers freely into it is a fundamental misunderstanding of the boss. Created awakened-one.md.
Power-heavy Silent decks (7 Powers in deck) are specifically bad against Awakened One. Deck building for Act 3 should factor in the visible boss.
Sozu is dangerous for boss fights that need emergency potion use.
Phase 2 strips all buffs -- Powers played in Phase 1 provide zero carry-over value, only the Str given to the boss mattered.
